rag_rules: Preserve the existing AADL architecture and modify only the owning declaration needed for the inserted annex block.
Keep owner and scope consistent: contract-style annex content belongs with component type scope, while implementation-level content belongs with implementation scope.
Do not move, rename, or alter unrelated features, subcomponents, connections, or properties when inserting an annex block.
generic_rules: Attach new content to the correct owning component or implementation without changing structural declarations outside that owner.
Maintain valid scope boundaries so inserted content does not depend on names that are only visible in a different owner.
Prefer minimal edits that preserve formatting and surrounding model structure.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AADL annex blocks use the form `annex agree {** ... **};`.
Implementation scopes come after the structural elements they depend on; type scopes should reference only type-visible features.
anti_patterns: Do not place type-scope contract content into an implementation owner just because that file section is convenient.
Do not introduce references to subcomponent paths or other implementation-only names into a type-owned contract block.
Do not perform broad refactoring or structural cleanup as part of annex insertion.
irrelevant_topics: Temporal equation syntax and logical operator details for clause generation
Scalar helper type rules for bool/int/real declarations
Requirement-specific assumption or guarantee content selection
